events.name: Germany CPI (Y-o-Y) (Mar)
events.description: Measures the annual change in prices for goods and services purchased by households in Europe's largest economy.

events.name: US Pending Home Sales (M-o-M) (Feb)
events.description: A leading indicator of housing market health, measuring the number of homes under contract to be sold.

events.name: China Manufacturing PMI (Mar)
events.description: A key survey of purchasing managers reflecting the health and expansion or contraction of China's industrial sector.

events.name: Eurozone CPI (Y-o-Y) (Mar)
events.description: The preliminary estimate of consumer price inflation across the Eurozone, used by the ECB for policy decisions.

events.name: US ADP Nonfarm Employment Change (Mar)
events.description: An estimate of private-sector payroll growth, often viewed as a precursor to the official government jobs report.

events.name: US ISM Manufacturing PMI (Mar)
events.description: Evaluates the performance of the US manufacturing sector based on production, new orders, and employment data points.

events.name: US Initial Jobless Claims
events.description: A weekly measure of new individuals filing for unemployment benefits, providing a real-time view of labor market stress.

events.name: US Trade Balance (Feb)
events.description: Measures the difference in value between imported and exported goods and services for the United States economy.

events.name: US Non-Farm Payrolls (Mar)
events.description: The most influential economic report, measuring total job creation in the US economy excluding the agricultural sector.

events.name: US Unemployment Rate (Mar)
events.description: The percentage of the total American workforce that was unemployed and actively seeking work during the month.
